examples: P45: 'Ink columns, wagon dates, place names' now turns on a colon before the depot list, which sounds more like Sylvanas sorting evidence than a writer stacking fragments.
P51: 'South and west—not toward one spoiled storehouse' uses the style-target em-dash turn instead of a hard stop that flattened the thought.
P69: combined Cyndia, Lyriel, and Jaina into one moving sentence so the saddle paragraph reads like formation awareness, not camera cuts.
P79-P85: folded the repeated short beats ('One strike was warning.' / 'No siege train. No cult banners.') into sharper, more continuous procedural rhythm.
P335: the closing map read now lands as one measured sentence instead of three scenic fragments, keeping Sylvanas's voice clipped and controlled.

notes: Opening clerk scene stays inside Sylvanas: she gets only what his eyes and hesitation give her, not his reasons.
Horseback analysis remains tactical and bodily; the narration never leaves Sylvanas's road read to announce Jaina's interiority.
Corpse scene passes because Jaina's certainty arrives through crystal work, expression, and spoken diagnosis, never through head-hop.
The naming shift to 'Jaina' is explicitly Sylvanas's internal register loosening, which fits the plan and stays in POV.
Old-seller recall remains memory-limited: coat, nose, voice, wagon slats. Sylvanas cannot suddenly recover a full face or hidden identity.
Chapter 4's endpoint holds: the same four-person party is already south of Millhaven and working the grain road at once.
Jaina's ingestion model is treated as established method, not rediscovered revelation; the chapter scales it up through paperwork.
Barov enters only as paperwork pressure and route overlap. The prose never upgrades the house name into solved guilt.
The old grain seller stays unresolved and partial, matching the memory file's patched coat, broad hat, broken nose, and practical road voice.
Thalassian horse handling remains consistent with the location note: light tack, disciplined road work, and quick settling under trained riders.

examples: P69: added one connective road sentence to break a short-sentence run in the travel paragraph.
P111: Crossbar's audit line now carries on one breath instead of tapping through five clipped inventory fragments.
P143: Millbrook Spur's empty-yard reveal compresses into a colon-led list, keeping force without telegram cadence.
P189-P215: the corpse read and cleanup deduction now use clause links and one semicolon, giving the office sequence human connective tissue.
P301-P341: the old-seller memory and route-choice close were recomposed into longer carrying sentences so the chapter ends on pressure, not note cards.
Chapter bridge passes: chapter4's first-light southwest departure resolves into an hour-south depot stop without recap drag.
Scene 1 to Scene 2 remains causal: the road widens, Sylvanas reads a live enemy, and the next depot is already cleaned.
The Crossbar 'South' tag hands directly into Millbrook Spur; no temporal throat-clearing breaks the pursuit.
The waystation analysis grows out of the cleaned depot scraps, body, and Barov seal; it does not feel like a new scene stapled on.
Chapter close points cleanly into chapter6: same four riders, southwest feeder road, Strahnbrad/Arthas pressure, Andorhal still ahead.
continuity.crossChapter: Passed — chapter5 inherits chapter4's road, urgency, cast, and grain-chain focus without reset.

examples: P85: clipped rhetorical fragments were tightened into one harder sentence so the paragraph stops posturing and keeps moving.
P195: 'Not fear.' became an em-dash turn, cutting a piece of explanatory scaffolding while preserving Sylvanas's read.
P223: the chalk-slate reveal now carries the cramped script and chased mark in one sentence instead of atomized staging.
P317: the old-seller memory no longer pauses on the isolated 'Still.' beat, which had started to sound like writerly throat-clearing.
P339: removed the closing simile; the route itself is enough.
P273: 'the duplicate routes have to live somewhere' -> 'copies of the route books have to live somewhere' — plainer, spoken, and tied to the paperwork on the table.
Checked Jaina's disease language in Scene 1: she says 'spread wider,' 'low dose,' and 'shipped grain longest' — technical enough for her, still speakable.
Checked the cleaned-depot exchange: 'cleaners scrubbing the road behind them' stays lived and visual, not bureaucratic.
Checked the Strahnbrad route debate: Jaina speaks in field pragmatics ('cleaner records,' 'lighter trail'), not report diction.
Checked Sylvanas's lines throughout: commands and questions remain blunt, tactical, and free of institutional jargon.
